prevalence.trend: increasing with obesity and sedentary lifestyle

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis: heel pain worst with first steps; tenderness at medial calcaneal tubercle; imaging reserved for atypical cases

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate with stretching, footwear, and physiotherapy

regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Stretching, activity modification, heel supports, NSAIDs; physiotherapy; steroid injection in selected cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to physiotherapy and orthotics in some SUS regions
